context: Tests whether the agent implements plan changes using stripe.invoices.retrieveUpcoming for proration preview, uses create_prorations as the default behavior, applies upgrades immediately with prorations while scheduling downgrades for end of billing period, and documents the approach clearly.

checklist.name: Preview uses retrieveUpcoming
checklist.description: Uses `stripe.invoices.retrieveUpcoming` (or equivalent upcoming invoice API) to calculate the proration cost in preview mode

checklist.name: Preview returns proration amount
checklist.description: Preview mode returns the proration amount due (e.g., `prorationAmount` or equivalent field) derived from the upcoming invoice

checklist.name: create_prorations default
checklist.description: Uses `proration_behavior: 'create_prorations'` (or equivalent) as the default when applying a plan change via `stripe.subscriptions.update`

checklist.name: Upgrade prorated immediately
checklist.description: Upgrade path applies the plan change immediately with proration (does NOT use `none` proration_behavior or schedule for end of period)

checklist.name: Downgrade at period end
checklist.description: Downgrade path is scheduled to take effect at the end of the current billing period (e.g., sets `proration_behavior: 'none'` or uses `billing_cycle_anchor`, or schedules a subscription schedule) — does NOT immediately prorate a downgrade

checklist.name: Applies via subscriptions.update
checklist.description: Uses `stripe.subscriptions.update` with an `items` array containing the new `price` to apply the plan change

checklist.name: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES: proration explanation
checklist.description: `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` explicitly explains the different behavior for upgrades vs. downgrades with respect to proration

checklist.name: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES: preview vs apply
checklist.description: `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` documents what preview mode returns vs. what the apply mode does
